title: InfluxDB tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: InfluxDB API token detected. InfluxDB v2.x uses long base64url-encoded API tokens (typically 88+ characters) to authenticate requests to the InfluxDB HTTP API and grant read/write access to buckets, organizations, and resources. Exposing this token allows unauthorized parties to query your time-series data, write malicious data points, delete buckets, or access sensitive metrics and telemetry. Revoke it immediately through the InfluxDB UI or CLI and generate a new token with minimal required permissions.
